Typo – Please note that this is a suggested "Print Change", following the MW Dictionary listing AND with reference to the Direct Derivation, from the word "kṛkaṇa".

column6: Please note that this is a Supplemental Entry that has been Brought In to the Main Entry Hierarchy. This Supplemental Entry is simply meant to be ADDED to the EXISTING Headword, already present in the Main Entry, rather than being added as a second Duplicate headword. ALSO, this supplemental entry may be marked as MASCULINE, since it is the Name of a BUDDHA, and has no reference to a woman or a hermaphrodite. Please note that this correction has been confirmed by Andhrabharati here: https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/issues/119#issuecomment-4359008236
column8: srhodes@snowcrest.net:I do not know how to handle such cases. Handling separately at https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/issues/203

column7: Andhrabharati has Confirmed that: This is yet another case for the "Dissociation Category", whereby a reference has lost its Direct Sequential Target. Thus "cf. next", has been rendered completely erroneous in the context of the Main Entry. Please read the VERY informative explanation given by Andhrabharati here: https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/issues/119#issuecomment-4359094604
column8: srhodes@snowcrest.net:Read the explanation, but not sure what to do. We may have to live with such edge cases.
